title: Cozy Irish Potato Pie
description: A malty, creamy potato pie with a buttery shortcrust, baked in a cast-iron skillet for a comforting, kosher-friendly meal.
instructions: Combine flour, butter, and ice water in a bowl and mix until dough forms; chill for 10 minutes.
Press dough into a 10-inch cast iron skillet to form a crust and bake for 5 minutes to set the base.
Boil potatoes in salted water until tender, about 10 minutes, then drain and mash with butter, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and malted milk powder.
Add milk and heavy cream to the mash and stir until smooth and creamy.
Pour the potato mixture into the baked crust, smoothing the top.
Bake the pie for 25 minutes or until the top is golden and the filling is set.
Remove from oven and let rest in the skillet for 5 minutes before serving.
Sprinkle chopped parsley over the top for a fresh finish.
